text: Railway: Vande Bharat Express. 136 trains operational. Semi-high speed: 160 km/h. Made in India by ICF Chennai. 52 routes across India. Tilting technology trains next. Chair car and Executive class. WiFi, GPS-based passenger info.

Railway: Mumbai-Ahmedabad High Speed Rail (Bullet Train). 508 km. ₹1.10 lakh crore. JICA funded (81%). Shinkansen E5 technology. 320 km/h speed. 12 stations. Travel time: 2 hours. Under construction. Target: 2028-29.

Railway: Dedicated Freight Corridors (DFC). Eastern DFC: 1,337 km (Ludhiana-Dankuni). Western DFC: 1,506 km (JNPT-Dadri). ₹1.2 lakh crore. 70% operational. Double-stack containers. Decongests passenger routes. ₹38,000 cr JICA loan.

Railway: Amrit Bharat Station Scheme. 1,309 stations being modernized. ₹24,470 crore. Roof plazas, improved facades, city integration. Green buildings, solar panels. Escalators, lifts, waiting lounges. Target completion: 2026.

Railway: Kavach (meaning 'armour'). Indian-developed Automatic Train Protection system. Anti-collision technology. Covers 6,000 km sanctioned. ₹14,000 crore investment. Prevents signal passing at danger. Automatic braking. SIL-4 safety standard.

Railway: Electrification. 95% of broad gauge network electrified (62,400 km). Target: 100% by 2025. Saves ₹15,000 crore diesel cost annually. Reduces carbon emissions. Electric loco production at CLW Chittaranjan — world's largest.
